 {"id":"bd-d84j","title":"Fix PR #319: Performance Improvements - CI failures and lint errors","description":"PR #319 (Performance Improvements) has excellent performance optimizations but is blocked by CI failures.

## The PR
- URL: https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/pull/319
- Author: @rsnodgrass (Ryan)
- Claimed improvements: bd ready 20.5x faster (752ms → 36.6ms), startup 10.5x faster

## CI Failures

### Lint Errors (8 total)
1. cmd/bd/deletion_tracking.go:57 - unchecked os.Remove
2. cmd/bd/import.go:548 - unchecked os.RemoveAll
3. cmd/bd/message.go:205 - unchecked resp.Body.Close
4. cmd/bd/migrate_issues.go:633 - unchecked fmt.Scanln
5. cmd/bd/migrate_issues.go:701 - unchecked MarkFlagRequired
6. cmd/bd/migrate_issues.go:702 - unchecked MarkFlagRequired
7. cmd/bd/show.go:610 - gosec G104 unhandled error
8. cmd/bd/show.go:614 - gosec G104 unhandled error

### Test Failures
All syncbranch_test.go tests failing with:
"migration external_ref_column failed: failed to create index on external_ref: sqlite3: SQL logic error: no such table: main.issues"

This suggests the PR branch needs rebasing on current main.

## Required Work

### 1. Fix Lint Errors
Add proper error handling for all 8 flagged locations. Most can use _ = or log warnings.

### 2. Rebase on Current Main
The migration test failures indicate the branch is out of sync. Need to:
- git fetch upstream
- git rebase upstream/main
- Resolve any conflicts
- Verify tests pass locally

### 3. Verify CI Passes
- All lint checks green
- All tests pass (Linux, Windows, Nix)

## Optional Improvements
- Consider splitting into smaller PRs (core index, WASM cache, testing infra)
- Add documentation for benchmark usage
- Extract helper functions in doctor/perf.go for better testability

## Value
This PR delivers real performance improvements. The index optimization alone is worth merging quickly once CI is fixed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-15T12:24:34.50322-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-15T12:43:11.49933-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-15T12:43:11.49933-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-d9e0","title":"Extract validation functions to validators.go","description":"Move validatePriority, validateStatus, validateIssueType, validateTitle, validateEstimatedMinutes, validateFieldUpdate to validators.go","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-01T19:28:54.915909-07:00","updated_at":"2025-11-02T12:32:00.159298-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-02T12:32:00.1593-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-d9mu","title":"Cross-rig external dependency support","description":"Support dependencies on issues in other rigs/repos.

## Use Case

Gas Town issues often depend on Beads issues (and vice versa). Currently we use labels like `external:beads/bd-xxx` as documentation, but:
- `bd blocked` doesn't recognize external deps
- `bd ready` doesn't filter them out
- No way to query cross-rig status

## Proposed UX

### Adding external deps
```bash
# New syntax for bd dep add
bd dep add gt-a07f external:beads:bd-kwjh

# Or maybe cleaner
bd dep add gt-a07f --external beads:bd-kwjh
```

### Showing blocked status
```bash
bd blocked
# → gt-a07f blocked by external:beads:bd-kwjh (unverified)

# With optional cross-rig query
bd blocked --resolve-external
# → gt-a07f blocked by external:beads:bd-kwjh (closed) ← unblocked!
```

### Storage
Could use:
- Special dependency type: `type: external`
- Label convention: `external:rig:id`
- New field: `external_deps: ["beads:bd-kwjh"]`

## Implementation Notes

Cross-rig queries would need:
- Known rig locations (config or discovery)
- Read-only beads access to external rigs
- Caching to avoid repeated queries

For MVP, just recognizing external deps and marking them as 'unverified' blockers would be valuable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T02:27:23.892706-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:32:49.261551-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T22:32:49.261551-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-u2sc","title":"GH#692: Code quality and refactoring improvements","description":"Epic for implementing refactoring suggestions from GitHub issue #692 (rsnodgrass). These are code quality improvements that don't change functionality but improve maintainability, type safety, and performance.

Original issue: https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/issues/692

High priority items:
1. Replace map[string]interface{} with typed structs for JSON output
2. Adopt slices.SortFunc instead of sort.Slice (Go 1.21+)
3. Split large files (sync.go, init.go, show.go)
4. Introduce slog for structured logging in daemon

Lower priority:
5. Further CLI helper extraction
6. Preallocate slices in hot paths
7. Polish items (error wrapping, table-driven parsing)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:26:31.630004-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T22:07:32.477628-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T22:07:32.477628-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-u2sc.1","title":"Replace map[string]interface{} with typed JSON response structs","description":"Many CLI commands use map[string]interface{} for JSON output which loses type safety and compile-time error detection.

Files with map[string]interface{}:
- cmd/bd/compact.go (10+ instances)
- cmd/bd/cleanup.go
- cmd/bd/daemons.go
- cmd/bd/daemon_lifecycle.go

Example fix:
```go
// Before
result := map[string]interface{}{
    "status": "ok",
    "count": 42,
}

// After
type CompactResponse struct {
    Status string `json:"status"`
    Count  int    `json:"count"`
}
result := CompactResponse{Status: "ok", Count: 42}
```

Benefits:
- Compile-time type checking
- IDE autocompletion
- Easier refactoring
- Self-documenting API","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:26:44.088548-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:48:22.88824-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:48:22.88824-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u2sc.1","depends_on_id":"bd-u2sc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:26:44.088931-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-u2sc.2","title":"Migrate sort.Slice to slices.SortFunc","description":"Go 1.21+ provides slices.SortFunc which is cleaner and slightly faster than sort.Slice.

Found 15+ instances of sort.Slice in:
- cmd/bd/autoflush.go
- cmd/bd/count.go
- cmd/bd/daemon_sync.go
- cmd/bd/doctor.go
- cmd/bd/export.go
- cmd/bd/import.go
- cmd/bd/integrity.go
- cmd/bd/jira.go
- cmd/bd/list.go
- cmd/bd/migrate_hash_ids.go
- cmd/bd/rename_prefix.go
- cmd/bd/show.go

Example migration:
```go
// Before
sort.Slice(issues, func(i, j int) bool {
    return issues[i].Priority < issues[j].Priority
})

// After
slices.SortFunc(issues, func(a, b *types.Issue) int {
    return cmp.Compare(a.Priority, b.Priority)
})
```

Benefits:
- Cleaner 3-way comparison
- Slightly better performance
- Modern idiomatic Go","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:26:55.573524-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:10:12.639807-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:10:12.639807-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u2sc.2","depends_on_id":"bd-u2sc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:26:55.573978-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
